# MESkit — The AI-Native MES
> An MES, but an Autonomous one. Open-source, ISA-95 aligned, simulation-first, agent-powered, MQTT-ready.

## What is MESkit
- [What is MESkit? (FAQ)](https://meskit.cloud/faq): Core definitions and product status
- [What is an AI-native MES?](https://meskit.cloud/blog/what-is-an-ai-native-mes): Category-defining explainer

## Product
- [Product overview](https://meskit.cloud/product): Build, Configure, Run, Monitor modes
- [AI agents](https://meskit.cloud/agents): Three specialized agents sharing one typed tool layer
- [Architecture](https://meskit.cloud/architecture): Four-layer design with tool layer rationale
- [ISA-95 mapping](https://meskit.cloud/isa-95): Standards alignment and database schema mapping

## Technical
- [How agents call MES tools](https://meskit.cloud/blog/how-meskit-agents-call-mes-tools): Tool registration and call chain deep-dive
- [From simulation to MQTT](https://meskit.cloud/blog/from-simulation-to-mqtt-same-tools-different-transport): Transport transition without logic rewrites
- [ISA-95 for developers](https://meskit.cloud/blog/isa-95-for-developers): Practical mapping from standard to Postgres schema
- [Roadmap](https://meskit.cloud/roadmap): M1-M6 milestone timeline

## Key facts
- Identity: The AI-Native MES — an MES, but an Autonomous one
- License: MIT (open source)
- Stack: Next.js, Supabase, Claude API (tool-use)
- Standards: ISA-95 aligned data model
- Agents: Operator Assistant, Quality Analyst, Production Planner
- North Star: Predict machine failure and reschedule autonomously via three AI layers (Sentinel, Strategist, Executor)
- Tool layer: 20+ typed Zod-validated server actions shared by UI and agents
- Database: 12 ISA-95-mapped Postgres tables via Supabase
- Status: Pre-M1 implementation with finalized architecture and public roadmap
